problem.summary: AES_encrypt on SG2044 (RVV 1.0 @ VLEN=128) runs entirely in scalar T-table mode with zero vector or crypto instructions. IPC=0.878, test_bio_enc duration=5.3s, 569 lines of pure scalar assembly. Each AES round requires 16 scalar lwu T-table lookups + 16 XOR operations forming a long dependency chain.
evidence: perf annotate: AES_encrypt (31 samples) shows zero v*/vaes* instructions; all scalar lwu/xor/addw/srl/and instructions
perf stat: branch_miss_rate=7.816% (elevated, T-table loop bgtz branches)
SG2044 hardware supports RVV 1.0 but AES code does not use vector or crypto instructions
OpenSSL has existing zvkned vector crypto AES paths: aes-riscv64-zvkned.pl and aes-riscv64-zvbb-zvkg-zvkned.pl in crypto/aes/asm/

rootCause.summary: Build configuration lacks zvkned crypto extension flag. AES_encrypt T-table implementation runs pure scalar. OpenSSL's existing vector crypto AES assembly (aes-riscv64-zvkned.pl) is never selected because the compiler and runtime dispatch cannot detect zvkned capability.
performanceFactors: IPC=0.878 (low for 3-wide OoO C920, T-table dependency chain limits ILP)
16 scalar lwu per round + 16 xor forming serial dependency
If zvkned available: one vaesem.vv/vaesef.vv replaces entire round loop, expected 3-5x speedup

mustPreserve: Backward compatibility: non-zvkned systems must fall back to scalar T-table
Runtime dispatch: crypto/riscvcap.c riscv_hwprobe() must correctly detect zvkned
Prior rv64gcv optimization: must not regress SHA-256 vector performance
Cross-compilation: change must not break non-RISC-V targets
